chunks.text: University of California at Berkeley Department of Electrical Engineering & Computer Sciences Instructional Support Group /share/b/pub/audio.help Oct 13 2008 CONTENTS Audio on the Solaris X86 (PC) work

2008 CONTENTS Audio on the Solaris X86 (PC) workstations Audio on the Solaris SPARC (SunRay) workstations Audio on the Windows systems ==> Note: Instructional workstations do not have external speakers, unless an instructor has req

ernal speakers, unless an instructor has requested them for a specific use. Students are advised to bring their own headphones. Audio on the Solaris X86 (PC) workstations ------------------------------------------ On the X86 systems

---------------------------- On the X86 systems running Solaris in 275 Soda, you can control the audio of the headphone jack with the command /usr/bin/gnome-volume-control For help: man gnome-volume-control On the DELL X86 systems

an gnome-volume-control On the DELL X86 systems in 275 Soda, you can plug headphones into the green audio input connector along the bottom of the back of the computer. (Unfortunately, you cannot use the more convenient connector on the front

not use the more convenient connector on the front of the computer.) Audio on the Solaris SPARC (SunRay) workstations ------------------------------------------------ On the SunRays xterminals running Solaris, you can control the audio of

s running Solaris, you can control the audio of the headphone jack with the command /opt/SUNWut/bin/utsettings For help: man -M /opt/SUNWut/man utsettings Audio on the Windows systems ---------------------------- The MS Windows systems

------------------------ The MS Windows systems typically have RealPlayer, CD Player, Volume Control and Media Player installed. Please contact "inst@eecs.berkeley.edu" if you have questions. EECS Instructional Support 384/3
